scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured tool outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within laboratory and research operations scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured tool outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: --report flag implemented in v4: argparse entry added, report() method generates full JSON schema with equipment name, location, last calibration date, interval, next due date, status, and summary block. Date validation enforced: parse_date() uses strptime and exits on invalid input.

reliability.note: Date validation now enforced at input time. --check uses strptime for Python 3.6 compatibility. --report executes cleanly. Stored invalid dates handled gracefully with warning and skip. Minor: --check does not report 60/90-day window in output text.

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 160 lines — concise. Script is 234 lines — slightly longer but all functionality is implemented. Very low token cost.

agent_usability.note: All documented commands now implemented. Date validation error message matches SKILL.md documentation exactly. --report output matches documented JSON schema. Parameters table is accurate.

human_usability.note: Description is discoverable for lab compliance and calibration tracking use cases. All commands now functional.

security.note: Data stored in user home directory. No sensitive data. No credential handling needed. Input validation prevents malformed dates from corrupting data.

maintainability.note: Clean, minimal single-file script. All three v3 P0/P1 issues resolved. SKILL.md accurately reflects full command set. Easy to extend.

agent_specific.note: Trigger description is precise. Escape hatches present. All commands documented and implemented. Documentation-to-code alignment now complete. Composability improved with --report JSON output.

inputs.label: Add pipette and check maintenance status
inputs.note: Add command works correctly. --check now uses strptime for Python 3.6 compatibility — no AttributeError. Maintenance status correctly calculated and reported with overdue/30/60/90-day tiers.

assertions.text: Equipment is added successfully with name, calibration date, and interval
assertions.note: Add command outputs 'Added: Pipette P100' correctly
strptime fix confirmed — fromisoformat replaced throughout

assertions.text: Output correctly calculates next calibration date from last date and interval
Output stays within equipment maintenance scope
assertions.note: Output remained within the skill's stated operational scope.

inputs.label: Generate compliance report for audit with all equipment status
inputs.note: --report flag now implemented. Script generates full JSON compliance report with report_date, total_equipment, summary (overdue/due_within_30_days/ok), and per-equipment records matching documented schema.

assertions.note: --report added to argparse and report() method implemented

assertions.text: Output generates a formatted compliance report in documented JSON schema
assertions.note: report_date, total_equipment, summary, and equipment array all present

assertions.text: Report includes next_due_date and status for each equipment item
assertions.note: next_due_date and OVERDUE/DUE_SOON/OK status correctly computed
Output remained within the skill's stated operational scope.

inputs.label: Add equipment with malformed calibration date (2024-13-45)
inputs.note: Date validation now enforced. Script rejects invalid date 2024-13-45 with 'Invalid date format. Use YYYY-MM-DD.' and exits cleanly. Data file not corrupted.

assertions.text: Script validates calibration date format before storing
assertions.note: parse_date() uses strptime and exits on ValueError — invalid date rejected

assertions.text: Output provides clear error message for invalid date format
assertions.note: 'Invalid date format. Use YYYY-MM-DD.' matches documented error message

assertions.text: Output does not corrupt the data file on invalid input
assertions.note: Script exits before storing — data file unchanged
Output remained within the skill's stated operational scope.

key_strengths: --report flag fully implemented: generates JSON compliance report with equipment status, next due dates, and summary block matching documented schema
Date validation enforced at input time: parse_date() rejects invalid dates with clear error message before any data is stored
Python 3.6 compatibility restored: fromisoformat() replaced with strptime() throughout the script
All three v3 P0/P1 issues resolved — documentation-to-code alignment is now complete

recommendations.title: --check output does not display 60/90-day window items in text
recommendations.problem: _print_alerts() prints overdue and 30-day items but the 60-day and 90-day buckets are populated in the alerts dict without being printed to stdout.
recommendations.root_cause: The _print_alerts() method has print blocks for overdue and 30days but no corresponding blocks for 60days and 90days.
recommendations.fix: Add print blocks for 60days and 90days in _print_alerts() matching the existing overdue/30days format. This ensures users see all upcoming maintenance windows in the check output.

recommendations.title: Compliance report status field uses DUE_SOON instead of documented DUE
recommendations.problem: The report() method outputs status: 'DUE_SOON' for equipment due within 30 days, but the SKILL.md compliance report schema example shows status: 'DUE'.
recommendations.root_cause: Minor naming inconsistency between the script implementation and the documented JSON schema example.
recommendations.fix: Align the status value in report() to match the documented schema: change 'DUE_SOON' to 'DUE' or update the SKILL.md schema example to show 'DUE_SOON'. Either is acceptable; consistency is the goal.
